mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and tighten financial conditions, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.2pp, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative median 60-day forward returns, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit compared to additive proxies.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days specifically during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL month-over-month change exceeds 0.5%, or the signal reverses direction in high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 300, elevated federal funds rates amplify real borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined high-rate and high-inflation environment yields positive 5-day forward returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and wage growth, which supports corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 60-day forward returns, or the Rank IC drops below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% quarter-over-quarter confirms robust aggregate demand and business investment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in >50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses 2.0%, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and decreases in the unemployment rate signal a synchronized supply-side recovery and labor market tightening, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative median 5-day returns, or the interaction term shows no predictive power in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.
